title: Apple stock sees slight bump following Q2 2026 earnings report
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: Apple stock rose slightly after Q2 2026 earnings report, gaining 5.35% in after-hours trading. The initial dip was followed by a sharp rebound. CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh expressed optimism about future demand.

content.introduction: Apple stock rose slightly after Q2 2026 earnings report, gaining 5.35% in after-hours trading. The initial dip was followed by a sharp rebound. CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh expressed optimism about future demand.
body:
## Market Overview

Apple stock sees slight bump following Q2 2026 earnings report. Following the release of Apple s earnings report for the second fiscal quarter of 2026, shares rose slightly in after-hours trading as the market digested the results and outlook for Q3 2026. Apple stock rises slightly following Q2 2026 earnings. Apple s stock rose as much as 5.35% after the company reported its Q2 2026 results and commented on them in the conference call that followed. After the results were released, shares initially dipped as much as 1.24%, before rebounding sharply to a gain of 5.36% and eventually settling around 1.86% by the end of after-hours trading. In the interim, Apple CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh held a conference call during which they commented on Apple s Q2 2026 performance and the outlook for the next few months. During the call, they struck an optimistic tone around continued demand across iPhone and Services, as well as the company s broader product roadmap. Following the release of Apple s earnings report for the second fiscal quarter of 2026, shares rose slightly in after-hours trading as the market digested the results and outlook for Q3 2026. Apple s stock rose as much as 5.35% after the company reported its Q2 2026 results and commented on them in the conference call that followed. When the bell rang, Apple stock closed at $271.35, up 0.44% on the day. After the results were released, shares initially dipped as much as 1.24%, before rebounding sharply to a gain of 5.36% and eventually settling around 1.86% by the end of after-hours trading. Apple's stock rose 5.36% after-hours following Q2 2026 earnings, settling at a 1.86% gain. The company reported strong iPhone sales and services revenue, but faced supply constraints on Mac models. The stock closed at $271.35, up 0.44% for the day. Apple stock sees slight bump following Q2 2026 earnings report Avatar for Marcus Mendes Following the release of Apple s earnings report for the second fiscal quarter of 2026, shares rose slightly in after-hours trading as the market digested the results and outlook for Q3 2026. Apple stock rises slightly following Q2 2026 earnings Apple s stock rose as much as 5.35% after the company reported its Q2 2026 results and commented on them in the conference call that followed. When the bell rang, Apple stock closed at $271.35, up 0.44% on the day. After the results were released, shares initially dipped as much as 1.24%, before rebounding sharply to a gain of 5.36% and eventually settling around 1.86% by the end of after-hours trading. In the interim, Apple CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh held a conference call during which they commented on Apple s Q2 2026 performance and the outlook for the next few months. During the call, they struck an optimistic tone around continued demand across iPhone and Services, as well as the company s broader product roadmap.

## Key Developments

However, they also made it clear that Apple is still facing meaningful supply constraints across several Mac models, driven by an unfortunate mix of stronger-than-expected demand and limited memory availability. He began covering Apple news in Brazilian media in 2012 and later broadened his focus to the wider tech industry, hosting a daily podcast for seven years. Market Reaction Following the earnings release, Apple s stock price increased by 0.38% in aftermarket trading, reaching $270.87. This rise is attributed to the company s strong financial performance and positive forward-looking statements. The stock remains within its 52-week range, with a high of $288.62 and a low of $193.25, indicating stable investor confidence. However, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is currently overvalued relative to its Fair Value a key consideration for investors evaluating entry points. For those tracking valuation metrics, Apple appears on the Most Overvalued stocks list. company growth rate, and for services, that impact was slightly more favorable. Our install base of over 2.5 billion active devices has reached another all-time high across all major product categories and geographic segments. Services revenue was $31 billion, up 16% year-over-year. We saw strong performance across the board with double-digit growth in the vast majority of the markets we track. Company gross margin was 49.3%, above the high end of our guidance range and up 110 basis points sequentially. Products gross margin was 38.7%, down 200 basis points sequentially. Services gross margin was 76.7%, up 20 basis points sequentially. Operating expenses landed at $18.9 billion, up 24% year-over-year. This was slightly above the high end of our guidance range due to a one-time expense in SG&A. 21.11 0.29% Dow 30 49,499.27 -152.87-0.31% Nasdaq 25,114.44 222.13 0.89% Russell 2000 2,812.82 12.92 0.46% VIX 16.99 0.10 0.59% Gold 4,625.60 -4.00-0.09% Bitcoin USD 78,302.60 1,649.74 2.15% Crude Oil 102.50 -2.57-2.45% Image 2: Yahoo Finance Apple stock rises after Q2 earnings top estimates on strong iPhone, China sales Image 3: Daniel Howley Daniel Howley Technology Editor Updated Fri, May 1, 2026 at 10:04 AM EDT 2 min read 95 AAPL 3.24% Trade Apple on CoinbaseTrading disclosure Trading disclosure ... Stock Price: Quote, Forecast, Splits & News (AAPL) $111.2B Beat 1.58% EPS (Adj.) $1.92 (est.) $2.01 Beat 4.69% 1d Price Move: Current Price: $271.35 Highlights Transcript Documents Apple reported Q2 fiscal year 2026 revenue of $111.2 billion, up 17% year over year and a March quarter record, with diluted EPS of $2.01, up 22% year over year, both above the high end of guidance despite supply constraints primarily on iPhone and Mac. iPhone revenue reached $57 billion, up 22% year over year and a March quarter record, driven by the iPhone 17 family, with customer satisfaction measured at 99% in the U.S. and double digit growth across most major markets including the U.S., Greater China, India, and Latin America. For Q3 fiscal year 2026 guidance, Apple expects total company revenue growth of 14% to 17% year over year, gross margin between 47.5% and 48.5%, and services revenue growth at a similar rate to Q2 after removing foreign exchange tailwinds, while noting supply constraints will primarily affect Mac models including Mac mini, Mac Studio, and MacBook Neo, with memory costs expected to have an increasing impact on margins beyond the June quarter.
